questions.question:
Select the pair that best expresses a relationship similar to that expressed in the pair:

water : pipe ::

A. cart : road
B. electricity : wire
C. sea : beach
D. music : instrument
questions.to_find: the pair that shares the same relationship as water : pipe

understand.plain: In the pair water : pipe, water is a substance (or energy carrier) that flows through a pipe — the pipe is the medium or channel that carries the water. We need to find a pair where the first item is similarly carried or transmitted through the second item. Electricity flows through a wire in exactly the same way that water flows through a pipe, making B the best match.

keywords.explain: A relationship between two pairs of words where the same logical connection holds for both pairs.
keywords.example: water : pipe :: electricity : wire — both pairs show a carrier moving through a channel.

keywords.explain: The physical path or conduit through which something is transmitted or transported.
keywords.example: A pipe is the channel for water; a wire is the channel for electricity.

keywords.explain: The movement of a substance or energy from one place to another through a defined path.
keywords.example: Water flows through a pipe; electric current flows through a wire.

understand.visual_alt: The analogy is purely conceptual: water flows through a pipe just as electricity flows through a wire.

given.aim: Identify which answer pair mirrors the relationship water : pipe

terms.meaning: Water is the substance that is conveyed through a pipe, which acts as its dedicated channel.
terms.example: Plumbing uses pipes to direct water from the source to the tap.
terms.connects: Establishes a carrier-to-channel relationship that must be replicated in the answer pair.

terms.meaning: A cart travels on a road; but a cart is a vehicle, not a substance transmitted through the road — the road does not carry the cart inside it.
terms.example: A cart rolls along the surface of a road, so the relationship is traveller-to-surface, not carrier-to-channel.

terms.meaning: Electric current is transmitted through a wire, exactly as water flows through a pipe.
terms.example: Power lines (wires) carry electricity from a generator to a home.
terms.connects: Same carrier-to-channel relationship as water : pipe — correct answer.

terms.meaning: Sea and beach are adjacent geographical features; music is produced by an instrument, not transmitted through it.
terms.example: A beach borders a sea; an instrument generates music rather than being the conduit for it.
terms.connects: Neither matches the carrier-to-channel relationship — eliminates C and D.

given.plan: Map the core relationship in water : pipe (substance flows through channel), then test each option against this pattern, keeping the pair that exhibits the same logical bond.

steps.title: Identify the relationship in the given pair
steps.formula_raw: X : Y => X is transmitted/flows through Y
steps.apply: water (X) flows through pipe (Y). Pipe is the conduit or channel for water. Relationship type: substance-flows-through-channel.
steps.note: The key is that water does not merely sit beside the pipe or produce the pipe — it is actively carried inside it.

steps.title: Test each option against the identified relationship
steps.formula_raw: Does X flow through / get transmitted through Y?
steps.apply:
A. cart : road — cart travels on top of road, not through it. Different relationship.
B. electricity : wire — electric current is transmitted through a wire, just as water flows through a pipe. Match!
C. sea : beach — a beach is the boundary of a sea, not its conduit. Different relationship.
D. music : instrument — an instrument produces music, it is not a channel for music. Different relationship.
steps.note: Only option B preserves the carrier-to-channel (flow-through) relationship.

questions.formula_note: This is a verbal analogy question; no mathematical formulas are used. The reasoning relies on identifying the logical relationship type (carrier-to-channel) and matching it across pairs.

questions.question:
Select the pair that best expresses a relationship similar to that expressed in the pair:

Medicine : Health ::

A. Science : Experiment
B. Wealth : Peace
C. Education : Knowledge
D. Money : Happiness
questions.to_find: the pair that shares the same relationship as Medicine : Health

understand.plain: In the pair Medicine : Health, medicine is the systematic field of study and practice whose direct, intended outcome is health. The relationship is: a field/discipline produces or leads to its specific, intrinsic outcome. Education is likewise a systematic field of study and practice whose direct intended outcome is knowledge, making C the best match. Options B and D show things that may correlate with outcomes (wealth may bring peace, money may bring happiness) but those outcomes are incidental, not the essential and definitive purpose of the field.

keywords.explain: A relationship where the first word is an organised discipline/practice and the second word is its specific, intended result.
keywords.example: Medicine aims directly at health; education aims directly at knowledge.

keywords.explain: The fundamental, defining goal of an activity — not a side-effect or possible by-product.
keywords.example: Health is the intrinsic purpose of medicine; knowledge is the intrinsic purpose of education.

keywords.explain: How precisely the logical bond between words in one pair matches the bond in the given pair.
keywords.example: Wealth and peace have a weaker, more contingent link than medicine and health.

understand.visual_alt: The analogy is conceptual: medicine is practiced to achieve health, just as education is pursued to achieve knowledge.

given.aim: Identify which answer pair mirrors the relationship Medicine : Health

terms.meaning: Medicine is the organised discipline and practice specifically aimed at achieving and maintaining health. Health is its direct, intended outcome.
terms.example: Doctors practise medicine to restore or preserve a patient's health.
terms.connects: Establishes a discipline-to-intrinsic-outcome relationship that must be replicated in the answer pair.

terms.meaning: An experiment is the method or tool used within science, not the outcome that science produces.
terms.example: Scientists conduct experiments to test hypotheses — experiment is a means, not an end.
terms.connects: Relationship is discipline-to-method, not discipline-to-outcome — eliminates A.

terms.meaning: Education is the organised practice of teaching and learning whose direct, intended outcome is knowledge.
terms.example: Students go through education to acquire knowledge and understanding.
terms.connects: Same discipline-to-intrinsic-outcome relationship as medicine : health — correct answer.

terms.term: Wealth : Peace / Money : Happiness (Options B and D)
terms.meaning: Wealth and money can sometimes lead to peace or happiness, but those are contingent by-products, not the defined purpose of wealth or money.
terms.example: Wealth is accumulated for financial security; happiness as a result is incidental, not guaranteed.
terms.connects: Weaker, contingent cause-effect links — do not match the precise discipline-to-outcome bond — eliminate B and D.

given.plan: Determine the core relationship in Medicine : Health (an organised discipline produces its essential, intended outcome), then evaluate each option to find the pair that best replicates this same precise bond.

steps.title: Identify the relationship in the given pair
steps.formula_raw: X : Y => X is a discipline/practice whose specific, intended outcome is Y
steps.apply: Medicine (X) is practised specifically to achieve Health (Y). Health is the direct, defining purpose of medicine. Relationship type: organised-discipline-to-its-essential-outcome.
steps.note: The bond is tighter than a mere cause-effect: Y must be the primary, intrinsic purpose of X, not just a possible by-product.

steps.title: Test each option against the identified relationship
steps.formula_raw: Is Y the specific, intended outcome/purpose of X?
steps.apply:
A. Science : Experiment — experiment is a method of science, not its outcome. Relationship is tool/method, not outcome. Eliminated.
B. Wealth : Peace — peace is a possible but not guaranteed or essential outcome of wealth. Contingent link. Eliminated.
C. Education : Knowledge — knowledge is the direct, essential outcome that education is designed to produce. Strong match!
D. Money : Happiness — happiness is an incidental, not essential, consequence of money. Contingent link. Eliminated.
steps.note: Option C mirrors the discipline-to-intrinsic-outcome relationship most precisely.

questions.formula_note: This is a verbal analogy question requiring no mathematical formulas; the reasoning centres on identifying a discipline-to-intrinsic-outcome relationship and selecting the pair that replicates it most accurately.
